acceptance.proof: One caller-owned bounded query line, nonzero return port, and query identifier encode into and parse from the exact independently specified three-line protocol-110 short-info query candidate with checked byte accounting, canonical decimal spellings, and one final LF.

Public-safe fixtures cover exact valid boundaries plus damaged header, forbidden query bytes, truncated lines and decimal fields, oversized payload/query text, leading-zero and plus-sign noncanonical decimals, zero port, overflow, extra bytes, and missing/final-extra LF rejection with typed first offsets.

The fixture provenance names only the official public LSL network-connectivity documentation and its displayed short-info query example; candidate policy and independently authored fixtures remain separate from observed endpoint or implementation evidence.

The crate remains dependency-free, feature-inert, unsafe-free, and without sockets, multicast, endpoint selection, discovery runtime, retries, clocks, provider/currentness evidence, interoperability claims, devices, or Manifold authority.

architecture_decision.deferred: short-info response document binding and discovery runtime
architecture_decision.deferred_reason: Responses require a separately specified wire envelope and later runtime ownership; sockets, multicast, endpoint selection, retries, clocks, and interoperability evidence remain closed.
architecture_decision.material_advance: Opens the first bounded protocol-110 byte-shape contract needed to form or inspect a discovery query while remaining entirely source-only and inert.
architecture_decision.selected: canonical short-info query payload encoding and parsing

commit_policy: Commit the bounded query payload contract separately from response XML, sockets, multicast, endpoint selection, discovery runtime, retries, clocks, provider evidence, interoperability, and authority.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route LSLC-002C query wire-shape ownership and explicit runtime/authority exclusions.
Describe the exact bounded query payload contract and evidence limits.
Review Rusty LSL public and Manifold boundaries; no skill mutation expected.

non_scope: Short-info response documents, full stream-info responses, query language semantics or evaluation, response correlation, endpoint addresses, service/data-port interpretation, reachability, or interoperability.

Sockets, datagrams, multicast or broadcast groups, interface selection, endpoint selection, discovery or resolver runtime, retries, cancellation, threads, clocks, timeouts, currentness, or provider acquisition.
Installed liblsl, rLSL, or other implementation source intake; external oracle execution; copied captures; dependencies, features, unsafe Rust, FFI, native libraries, devices, or hidden activation.
Manifold admission, registry, subscription, route, lease, revision, replay, authorization, audit, session, topology, command, transport, product, or provider authority.
objective: Implement a dependency-free, byte-bounded canonical protocol-110 short-info query payload encoder/parser from public specification evidence, with exact field and total limits and typed rejection of damaged, truncated, oversized, and noncanonical inputs, while preserving source-only inert behavior.
outputs: A bounded owned short-info query value plus canonical encoder and borrowed parser with typed byte-offset failures.
Independent public-safe valid, damaged, truncated, oversized, and noncanonical fixtures bound to public documentation provenance.
Focused validation and public documentation stating exactly what local byte-shape admission proves and excludes.
